title: Go DB-only write expansion gate

stabilization_decision.reason: 23.4 proves only local/copied-DB flag-gated parity. Live write routing would require a separate Caddy/systemd/production DB/CSRF/rollback gate and must not be treated as automatic Go ownership.

schema_doc_discrepancy_gate.subject: Tags.name NOCASE schema/documentation discrepancy
schema_doc_discrepancy_gate.docs_claim: docs/SCHEMA.md documents Tags.name as UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE and documents idx_tags_name ON Tags(name COLLATE NOCASE).
runtime_truth: migrations/test DB currently create Tags.name as TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL without an explicit COLLATE NOCASE clause
routes/tags.py duplicate checks use exact SELECT id FROM Tags WHERE name = ? semantics
23.4 Go parity intentionally matched current Python exact-name duplicate behavior
blocked_until_resolved: Any broader tag CUD ownership that depends on name uniqueness semantics
not_authorized_in_23_5: docs/SCHEMA.md rewrite that claims a runtime correction was performed

why_selected: DB-only top-level route with no file, cascade, bulk, process, or nested /api/notes matcher expansion
Updates an existing single Categories row instead of creating a new identity
Avoids the unresolved Tags.name NOCASE discrepancy

candidate_matrix.reason: DB-only, but creates a new identity and computes max sort_order; category_update is the smaller next proof.
Migrates Notes.category_id, protects default categories, and has broader data movement than a single-row update.
DB-only, but nested /api/notes/... action routing intersects the hardened live matcher boundary; keep it behind an explicit later route gate.
Deletes Tags row and depends on tag ownership semantics while Tags.name discrepancy is unresolved.
Rewrites Note_Tags and deletes source Tags in one transaction; too broad for the next DB-only proof.
Duplicate creates rows and copies relationships; reorder is bulk; batch routes may couple to file deletion. These are not the smallest next route class.
Filesystem, backup, process, config, import/export, or server-local side effects remain outside DB-only write expansion.

next_implementation_contract.title: Second Go DB-only write implementation subgate
next_implementation_contract.scope: Local/copied-DB only. Do not change live Caddy/systemd/frontend defaults or production DB. Keep live category update Python-owned until a later explicit live routing gate.
next_implementation_contract.expected_enablement: Use an explicit local flag such as --enable-category-write / PRISM_GO_ENABLE_CATEGORY_WRITE=1; default Go runtime remains GET read-only with SQLite query_only = ON.
request_response_parity: Missing category id returns 404 with current Python response shape
Missing JSON body is accepted only if current Python behavior accepts it; otherwise match Python exactly
name/icon/sort_order omitted fields preserve existing values
trimmed empty name returns 400 Category name cannot be empty after 23.5-next.2 Python+Go hardening
duplicate category name returns 409 using current Python exact-name semantics
transaction_and_state_invariants: Update exactly one Categories row on success
No file, attachment, import/export, cleanup, server, or migration side effects
Rollback leaves Categories unchanged on validation or SQL failure
fixture_plan: Python vs Go response parity for name-only, icon-only, sort_order-only, combined update, missing id, empty-name 400, duplicate name
DB-state parity snapshot for Categories before/after each case
Default Go runtime rejects PUT without the explicit category-write flag
stop_conditions: Any need to edit live Caddy/systemd or production DB
Any schema migration or Tags.name uniqueness semantics change
Any file or attachment side effect

not_authorized_by_23_5: Tag delete or merge Go ownership

allowed_next_step.title: Second Go DB-only write implementation subgate
allowed_next_step.scope: Implement only the selected local/copied-DB category update candidate with parity and rollback tests; live routing remains blocked.
